title: P69-10 fix: in hosteddashboard, delete the array element ...
review_summary: Reviewer approved the plan for Tomonkyo and execution can proceed.
request_text: P69-10 fix: in hosteddashboard, delete the array element with id "master1" from src/data/mockUsers.js, and in src/pages/Dashboard.js move the top-level localStorage.getItem currentUser read into a useEffect inside the Dashboard component. Touch only those two files. Do not create new files. Apply the patch.

translation_json.normalized_request: In src/data/mockUsers.js remove the array element with id 'master1'. In src/pages/Dashboard.js refactor the top-level localStorage.getItem('currentUser') to be read inside a useEffect hook within the Dashboard component. Modify only these two files.
translation_json.intent: Apply targeted code cleanup patches to remove master1 user entry and refactor localStorage read to useEffect pattern
translation_json.objective: Remove 'master1' user from mockUsers.js array and move localStorage.getItem('currentUser') from top-level into useEffect inside Dashboard component
requested_outputs: Modified src/data/mockUsers.js with master1 entry removed from array
Modified src/pages/Dashboard.js with localStorage read inside useEffect

plan_json.objective: Implement the Jira issue by generating code changes, applying patches, running tests, and reviewing results.
plan_json.request_summary: P69-10 fix: in hosteddashboard, delete the array element with id "master1" from src/data/mockUsers.js, and in src/pag...
plan_json.change_summary: Remove 'master1' user entry from mockUsers.js array and refactor localStorage.getItem('currentUser') to be read inside a useEffect hook in Dashboard.js
plan_json.change_explanation: This patch addresses the data cleanup requirement by removing the hardcoded 'master1' user entry and moving the localStorage read for currentUser from module-level to component-level useEffect to avoid potential hydration issues.
assumptions: The referenced Jira issue exists and contains enough context to generate code.
Affected source files can be read from the knowledge index or sandbox.

tools.purpose: Generate code changes that implement the Jira issue requirements.

steps.title: Analyze Jira issue P69-10 and identify affected files
steps.expected_output: Identified two files requiring modification: src/data/mockUsers.js (remove master1 entry) and src/pages/Dashboard.js (move localStorage read to useEffect)
steps.success_criteria: Analysis confirms exact locations of master1 user entry and top-level localStorage.getItem call; no other files need modification

steps.title: Generate code patch for mockUsers.js and Dashboard.js
steps.expected_output: Generated patch removing { id: 'master1', ... } from mockUsers array and refactoring Dashboard.js to read localStorage.getItem('currentUser') inside useEffect with proper state management
steps.success_criteria: Patch correctly targets only the two specified files with exact changes: master1 deletion and useEffect refactor for localStorage

steps.title: Apply patch and run tests in sandbox
steps.expected_output: Patch applied successfully to both files; unit tests pass confirming no regressions in user data and Dashboard component behavior
steps.success_criteria: Both files modified correctly; test suite passes for Dashboard component and user management modules

steps.title: Review generated changes
steps.expected_output: Verified that mockUsers.js no longer contains master1 entry and Dashboard.js uses useEffect pattern for localStorage access; no unintended side effects
steps.success_criteria: Code review confirms clean implementation of both changes; no other files touched; patch is minimal and focused

review_json.summary: Reviewer approved the plan for Tomonkyo and execution can proceed.
